operation: First, a 2D sketch is initialized on the XY workplane: starting at coordinate (0, 0), draw a 5-unit long straight horizontal line to (5, 0), add a tangent arc ending at point (5, 5), draw a 5-unit long straight horizontal line left to (0, 5), then close the path to form a closed 2D profile. This profile is then extruded 3 units perpendicular to the XY workplane to produce the final 3D solid.
geometry: This is a uniform prismatic 3D solid with a consistent cross-section along its extrusion axis. Its cross-section has three straight edges: two equal-length parallel horizontal edges connected at one end by a straight vertical edge, and a curved quarter-circle edge connecting the opposite ends of the two horizontal edges. It has flat parallel top and bottom faces, with side faces that are either flat perpendicular to the cross-section plane or a continuous curved face matching the profile's rounded edge.
function: This is a simple prismatic spacer, mounting bracket, or assembly insert component with one rounded edge to eliminate sharp corners or fit against curved mating surfaces, with a simple geometry that is compatible with low-cost manufacturing methods including 3D printing, stamping, or CNC machining.
